Q6 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider a system with seven processes A through G and six resources R through W.
Resource ownership is as follows:
• Process A holds R and wants T.
• Process B holds nothing but wants T.
• Process C holds nothing but wants S.
• Process D holds U and wants S and T.
• Process E holds T and wants V.
• Process F holds W and wants S.
• Process G holds V and wants U.
The system was found in deadlock, which processes is/are deadlocked?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)

Q23 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A disk has 200 cylinders numbered from 0 to 199. The current head position is at cylinder 84 and the previous request was at 99, so the direction of head movement is towards lower cylinder number. The queue of pending disk request (in order of arrival) is [9, 122, 33, 61, 177, 84, 89, 149,
37]. Each cylinder movement takes 0.5 msec and every request serviced takes an addition 1.5 msec.
What is the total time (in sec) taken by C-SCAN for servicing given request.

NAT
Correct answer: 0.21
Solution

0.21

 
Q24 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider a system with the below resource allocation graph:

 
P1, P2 and P3 are the processes holding some instances of resources R1, R2 and R3.
Which of the following is/are incorrect statement(s)?
Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
• Given system is not in deadlock.
• There are multiple safe sequence possible start with process P1 or P3.
• There is a circular wait but no hold-wait in the given system.

Q25 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Match the given items in List-I with their corresponding matches in List-II:

 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Contiguous and indexed allocation support both direct and sequential access, whereas linked
allocation supports only sequential access to the file.
Therefore (c) is the answer.

Q27 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
(a) Parent and child can execute the same program.
(b) Child creation make copy of physical memory. Also consider exec( ) call.

Q28 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding allocation methods.
S1 : Both sequential and direct access can be supported by contiguous allocation.
S2 : The pointer overhead for linked allocation is greater then indexed allocation.
Which of the above statements are not true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Contiguous allocation allows sequential and direct access.
• Pointer overhead is higher in indexed allocation than linked allocation method.

Q32 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following implementation of the reader-writers problem. Note that mutex and db
are shared binary semaphores, both initialized to 1.

 
What can be said about the above implementation?
Correct answer: A
Solution

(c)
The first line any reader access is down(mutex). Now mutex is taken up by this reader and is not
released until this reader is done accessing the database. So multiple readers are not allowed into
the database at the same time, even though no writer is interested in getting access to the database.
So option (c) is the correct answer.
